blurb: Coffee with a Cop is back, and it's exactly what it sounds like. Pull up a chair with Cobb County Sheriff's Office deputies, grab a donut, and ask whatever is on your mind. No agenda, no presentations, just an open conversation with the people who work your community. It's a small thing that makes a real difference in how neighbors and law enforcement actually get to know each other.
scoring_notes: East Cobb homeowners tend to care about community safety and local civic engagement, so this fits thematically. However, the Senior Wellness Center venue signals this is aimed at an older demographic, and 2pm on a Tuesday is a tough time for working adults. Scored lower on uniqueness because Coffee with a Cop is a recurring national program format, not a one-time event. Better as a calendar listing than a featured event.

event_name: The Interface: Tech and Brunch Experience
blurb: If you work in tech or have been thinking about making a move in that direction, this is a free Sunday afternoon worth showing up for. The Interface brings together professionals from across Atlanta's tech world for a mixer with brunch at Timeless Kitchen in Marietta. It's casual, it's free, and it's right in your backyard. Good food, good conversation, and the kind of room where you might actually meet someone useful.
scoring_notes: East Cobb's high-income, educated demographic includes a solid chunk of tech and professional workers. Free, Sunday brunch format lowers the barrier to entry. However, the networking mixer format skews younger and more professionally ambitious than the typical 42-year-old East Cobb empty nester. Scored lower on audience match because it fits a subset of the audience rather than the core. Note: the listed start time of 12:00 AM appears to be a data error and should be confirmed as 12:00 PM.

blurb: If you have ever driven down Georgia's coast and wondered about those barrier island lighthouses, here is a full hour dedicated to the stories behind them. The Georgia Room at Switzer Library is hosting author William Rawlings for a lecture on the five surviving lighthouse structures along Georgia's coast, from the St. Marys River all the way to the Savannah River. It's history, it's beautiful photography, and it's free. Worth a Monday night.
scoring_notes: East Cobb's educated, 42-median-age homeowner crowd tends to enjoy local history and cultural programming. Georgia coastal history is broadly appealing. The author guest makes it feel more special than a standard library talk. Docked on uniqueness because it's a library lecture, which is common, and the 10pm listed time looks like a data error (likely 10am or 6pm) — real start time should be confirmed before publishing.

blurb: This is a genuinely fun Saturday afternoon activity and you will walk away with something growing on your kitchen counter. Gritters Library is hosting a hands-on workshop where you'll cultivate your own oyster mushroom garden from scratch. It's free, it takes a couple of hours, and it's the kind of thing that sounds unusual enough to actually be memorable. If you've got a teenager who's been glued to a screen all week, this is an easy yes.
scoring_notes: East Cobb has a strong homeowner, educated demographic that tends to skew toward gardening, cooking, and DIY activities. Oyster mushroom cultivation is niche enough to feel special and hands-on enough to be genuinely engaging. Free, Saturday afternoon, family-friendly but interesting to adults too. Loses a point on audience match because it is a library program and may not pull the weekend crowd competing with Memorial Day weekend hangover plans.

event_name: Classic Love: Music of Gerald LeVert
blurb: If you grew up with Gerald LeVert on the radio, this one is going to hit. Quinn takes the Timeless Music Series stage Thursday night for "Classic Love," a full tribute to one of R&B's smoothest voices. Timeless Kitchen is a small, intimate room, which means no bad seats and a lot of good energy. This is date night material, no question. Grab your tickets before the week gets away from you.
scoring_notes: East Cobb skews 42 median age, so Gerald LeVert is squarely in the sweet spot for adults who grew up in the 80s and 90s. Intimate venue, Thursday night, date-night vibe. Not a free show but income level makes ticket price a non-issue. Loses a point on demographic fit because it skews more urban R&B than the typical East Cobb taste profile, but uniqueness is high since live R&B tribute nights at this venue are not a weekly occurrence.

blurb:
Smith-Gilbert Gardens is celebrating the 10-year anniversary of its signature butterfly and pollinator exhibit, A Garden with Wings, with a full-day festival this Saturday.

Pollinator Palooza runs from 10am to 5pm and brings together gardens, education, and the kind of unhurried Saturday that's hard to manufacture. Smith-Gilbert is a genuine gem that a lot of people in this area have never been to, even though it's right up the road. This is a great reason to finally go.

It works for families with younger kids, for couples who want a beautiful day outside, and for anyone who just wants to slow down for a few hours. Ten years of this exhibit is worth celebrating. Check their website for admission details and plan to stay a while.
scoring_notes: The 10-year anniversary milestone makes this a more special version of what is normally a recurring seasonal event. Smith-Gilbert Gardens appeals to East Cobb's educated, nature-appreciating demographic. Works for families with younger kids or teens as well as empty nesters. Proximity is a plus. Slightly lower audience match since the core audience skews toward families with younger children more than East Cobb's aging-up demographic.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sundown Social Series – Sandy Springs**
📅 Returning this season | Sandy Springs
🎟️ Details at the link | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/24/the-future-of-sandy-springs)

The Sundown Social is back in Sandy Springs. It's an outdoor evening series — the kind of thing you show up to not quite knowing who you'll run into, and end up staying two hours longer than you planned.

For a neighborhood that's more young professionals and adults than it is school-night-bedtime households, this is exactly the right kind of event. Outdoor, social, low-commitment. You can walk in solo and leave with plans for the weekend.

Check Rough Draft Atlanta for the full schedule and dates so you don't miss the first one.
scoring_notes: The Sundown Social fits the adult-leaning, social Perimeter audience well. The series format is a slight knock on uniqueness — it's recurring — but the season return makes it timely for a newsletter publishing this week. Good match for young professionals and empty nesters who want something low-key and walkable. Lower uniqueness score reflects that it's a returning series rather than a standalone event, but the audience match is among the strongest of any candidate.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Juneteenth Celebration – City Springs**
📅 Coming to City Springs, Sandy Springs
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/27/juneteenth-celebration-city-springs/)

City Springs just announced this year's Juneteenth Celebration is coming back — and it's bigger. The event features live performances, local vendors, and expanded educational experiences that are new this year.

Juneteenth has grown into one of the most meaningful civic celebrations on the calendar, and City Springs is one of the best outdoor venues in the area to experience it. Free admission, walkable location, and a lineup built around community — it's the kind of event that reminds you why you like living here.

Details are still rolling out, so check cityssprings.com to mark your calendar before the full schedule fills in.
scoring_notes: Solid community event at a neighborhood anchor venue. Free admission removes any price barrier for an audience that wouldn't be deterred by cost anyway. The 'expanded' element gives it a fresh angle over prior years. Broad appeal across the adult and family mix in the Perimeter area. Slightly lower uniqueness score because Juneteenth events are widespread in metro Atlanta — the City Springs angle and local proximity is what elevates it for this newsletter specifically.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sandy Springs Invitational Car Show**
📅 Sunday, April 19 | Sandy Springs
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/11/artemis-ii-splashdown/)

If you've got even a passing interest in cars — classic, exotic, or just something you've never seen up close — this is worth your Sunday afternoon. The Sandy Springs Police Foundation is hosting its Invitational Car Show today, with over a hundred cars on display ranging from vintage classics to head-turning exotics. It's free, it's outside, and the proceeds support local police. It's also the kind of low-key, walk-around event that's easy to do with a coffee in hand, a kid on your hip, or just a couple of hours to kill before dinner. No tickets, no pressure — just cars and community.
scoring_notes: Happening today (April 19), so it's immediate and relevant for a newsletter publishing this week only if it goes out Sunday morning — otherwise this is stale. Good fit for the adult-skewing Perimeter audience. Free, community-benefit angle plays well with homeowners and professionals. Car shows skew male but the charity tie-in and outdoor format broaden the appeal. Uniqueness is moderate — it's a car show, but the invitational format and police foundation angle give it a purpose beyond just cars.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Dunwoody Art Festival**
📅 May 9-10 | Various times | Dunwoody
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://festivalguidesandreviews.com/georgia-festivals/)

Mark your calendars for the Dunwoody Art Festival next month — it's one of those rare events that manages to be both sophisticated and completely accessible. Local and regional artists will showcase everything from paintings and photography to jewelry and ceramics, all set up right in the heart of Dunwoody. This isn't your typical craft fair. The quality bar is high, the crowd is engaged, and you might actually find something worth hanging on your wall. With 70% of Perimeter residents holding bachelor's degrees or higher, this audience appreciates good art and has the disposable income to buy it. It's free to attend, runs all weekend, and gives you a chance to discover new artists while supporting the local arts community. Plus, you can walk there.
scoring_notes: Excellent demographic fit - highly educated, affluent audience that appreciates arts and culture. Annual event but still worthwhile for its quality and local relevance. Appeals to adult sophistication while remaining family-friendly. Free admission removes any barrier.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Atlanta Dogwood Festival**
📅 Saturday, April 10 | All Day | Piedmont Park, Atlanta
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/10/too-loud/)

The 90th Annual Atlanta Dogwood Festival is happening this weekend, and that number tells you something. This festival has staying power because it delivers what Atlanta springs are all about — perfect weather, blooming trees, and the whole city coming out to celebrate. Piedmont Park transforms into a massive outdoor party with live music on multiple stages, local artists selling their work, and food vendors serving everything from barbecue to funnel cakes. The Kid's Alley keeps families happy with activities and games. What sets this apart from smaller neighborhood festivals is the scale and quality — national touring acts, serious local musicians, and vendors who've earned their spot over decades. It's a 20-minute drive from Perimeter, but you're getting Atlanta's premier spring festival for free.
scoring_notes: Major Atlanta institution with 90-year history creates strong uniqueness score. Appeals to educated, culturally-engaged demographic. Free admission removes price barrier. Distance from Perimeter and large crowds may be deterrents for some suburban residents.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Sandy Springs Invitational Car Show**
📅 Saturday, April 19 | All Day | Sandy Springs
🎟️ $15 | [Get Tickets](https://roughdraftatlanta.com/2026/04/12/sandy-springs-car-show-fundraiser/)

This isn't your typical parking lot car show. The Sandy Springs Invitational brings together classic muscle cars, exotic sports cars, and everything in between for a full day of automotive eye candy. What makes it worth the drive is the mix — you'll see a 1967 Camaro parked next to a brand-new McLaren. The event benefits the Sandy Springs Police Foundation, so your ticket helps fund community programs while you browse some serious horsepower. Food trucks handle lunch, and there's usually live music to keep the energy up. It's the kind of Saturday where you plan to stay an hour and end up there all afternoon. Car enthusiast or not, there's something satisfying about seeing these machines up close.
scoring_notes: Strong appeal for Perimeter's affluent demographic with interest in luxury items. Annual event but unique enough to be noteworthy. $15 price point is very accessible for $105K median income households. Mix of classic and exotic cars appeals to broad age range.

blurb:
⭐ **Featured Event: Lemonade Days Festival**
📅 Wednesday, April 22 | All Day | Downtown Dunwoody
🎟️ Free | [Learn More](https://www.atlantaonthecheap.com/lemonade-days-festival-dunwoody/)

Dunwoody's biggest party of the year kicks off Wednesday and runs through Sunday. This is the 26th year of Lemonade Days, which means they've figured out how to do it right. Five straight days of live music, food trucks, local vendors, and activities that work for everyone from toddlers to grandparents. The festival takes over downtown Dunwoody, so you can walk between stages, grab dinner, and let the kids run around without driving anywhere. It's the kind of community event that makes you remember why you moved to the suburbs in the first place. The lineup changes daily, so you can come back multiple times and catch something different each night.
scoring_notes: Perfect fit for Perimeter's mixed demographic of young professionals and families. Five-day duration creates multiple opportunities to attend. Annual tradition with strong community appeal that matches suburban lifestyle preferences.
